feed.title: SAP Community - Cloud Integration
feed.subtitle: Cloud Integration Q&A in SAP Community

entry.title: Compensation Statement integration with FileNet using SAP CPI
entry.content:
Hello Community,

We have a client requesting Compensation Statement integration with FileNet using SAP CPI. 

Do you know if this is possible? And if not, could you suggest a workaround.

Best Regards,
Pavan

SAP SuccessFactors Compensation  SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central  SAP Integration Suite 

 

 

 

 

#

entry.title: SAP Integration suite
entry.content:
Hi community, is there any way to connect SAP integration cloud tenant with the servers(web socket supported) through websocket communication ?

Regards,

Mohan Sai

entry.title: Send/Receive mail with XOAUTH using CPI oder SAP BUILD (unattended bot)
entry.content:
We want to implement a scenario where we can send and receive mails from a Windows 365 Mail-Account.
Our security guideline specifies that we have to use XOAUTH to connect to the Mail-Account.
My question is if SAP CPI / SAP BUILD (unattended bot) are able to use XOAUTH or can we only use OAUTH2.

Where can I find examples for an implementation of XOAUTH using CPI and/or an unattended bot.

Thanks
Arnfried

entry.title: ADP and EC Integration using ADP Workforce Now
entry.content:
One of my client's USA-based subsidiary implemented ADP Workforce Now as its HCM solution last year. As a result, they plan to establish data integration between ADP and SAP SuccessFactors (SFs) this year.

There is already an API available for data integration between ADP and SFs, which appears to facilitate this process:
ADP Workforce Now - SAP SuccessFactors Integration

Request any information:
Could you introduce us to any case studies of other companies that have built data integration between ADP Workforce Now and SFs using the above API? Specifically, we would like to understand the following:

  • Detailed API specifications and the configuration tasks required on both the ADP and SFs sides.
  • Estimated costs and project timeline for the integration.
  • Potential concerns or challenges associated with the integration.

Appreciate any insights on this topics. Thanks!

entry.title: “Communication scenario Communication scenario SAP_COM_0070 is not in is not in scope"
entry.content:
I am trying to connect SAP and Concur.. i get the following error  “Communication scenario Communication scenario SAP_COM_0070 is not in is not in scope" 

Any documentation how to resolve this error ? 

Thanks..

entry.title: Hi All,Is there any proper documentation for Groovy for CPI by SAP ?
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

I was wondering if SAP has some official documentation for Groovy for CPI ? Or just learning groovy in general will do the trick?

I am relatively new to the SAP world (coming from IBM products world), so curious if there is any proper official documentation by SAP dedicated to the Groovy for SAP CPI.

#groovy #sapcpi #cpi #intigrationsuit

entry.title: Compensation Statements integration with FileNet using SAP CPI
entry.content:
Hi Community,

Compensation statements, generated and accessible within the SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central under Compensation portlet, are available for all employees enrolled in a compensation plan. We want to transfer these statements from SAP SuccessFactors to FileNet using SAP Cloud Platform Integration.

We are currently unable to identify the backend entity associated with the compensation statements within SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central (EC). Could you kindly provide guidance on how to fetch the compensation statements from SAP SuccessFactors EC. Could you kindly advise if there is any workaround solution to facilitate the integration from SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central (EC) to FileNet?

Thanks & Best Regards,
Pavan


Cloud Integration SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central

entry.title: ABAP Program to Upload PDF Form to OneDrive
entry.content:
Dear Experts,

I have a shared link to OneDrive folder. I need to upload PDF form into that folder by using shared link of OneDrive.

How can I achieve uploading PDF file in OneDrive folder using the shared link?

Your help will be highly appreciated.

Thank you.

Best Regards,

Muhammad Fahim

entry.title: Error when migrating PI/PO interface to Cloud Integration
entry.content:
Hello experts,

I’m working on a project to migrate interfaces (from PI to CI). After performing the migration, the iFlow is created normally. However, when I try to access the iFlow, I receive the following error:
"No artifact file (.iflw) found. Add a file to the project."

This is an inbound integration, meaning SAP receives the request. I have already migrated many other interfaces with the same scenario, and they worked fine.

Has anyone experienced this issue before?
I appreciate any help in advance.

Best regards,
Matheus Gomes

entry.title: Problem Statement: HTTP 403 Error in SAP CPI IFlow While Calling SAP Public Cloud OData API
entry.content:
Hello SAP Community,

I have designed an SAP CPI IFlow to call the API_MANAGE_WORKFORCE_TIMESHEET OData service in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud. However, I am receiving an HTTP 403 Forbidden error when invoking the API.

IFlow Overview:

Steps Implemented:

  1. Get CSRF Token – Successfully fetched via a GET request.
  2. Modify Headers – Added "x-csrf-token" and "Content-Type": "application/json".
  3. Authentication – Using Basic Authentication with a Communication User.
  4. Validated Payload – JSON format is correct as per API metadata.

Issue:

  • The API responds with 403 Forbidden.

Observations:

  1. 403 Forbidden Error – The request is reaching the API but is being denied access.
  2. CSRF Token Retrieved Successfully – Indicates that token handling is correctly implemented.
  3. Basic Authentication Used – The Communication User may lack required roles or permissions.
  4. Headers Properly Set – "x-csrf-token" and "Content-Type": "application/json" are included.
  5. Correct Payload Format – Ensured as per API metadata.
  6. Tested in Postman – Working Fine – The same API call with the same credentials works in Postman but fails in SAP CPI.

Questions:

  1. Are there any additional roles required for the Communication User in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud?
  2. Does this API require OAuth authentication instead of Basic Authentication when called from CPI?
  3. Are there any specific CPI security settings or firewall restrictions that could be blocking the request?
  4. Is there any difference in authentication behaviour between Postman and SAP CPI when calling this API?
  5. Does CPI require any special configurations for calling public cloud OData APIs (e.g., certificates, destinations, or policies)?

entry.title: Unable to update API Error while applying SuccessFactors OAuth connection API Management policy
entry.content:
Hey Peeps!

What am I trying to do - 

I am trying to set up SuccessFactors OAuth connection API Management policy for a Compound Employee SOAP call so that I don't need to configure any authentication credentials in Cloud Integration. 

 

What have I done till now -

- I copied over the 'SuccessFactors_OAuth2SAMLAssertion' policy template from 'Connect to SAP SuccessFactors' policy package from the Integration Suite Discover portal

 

- I copied the 'Compound Employee API' API from the 'SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central' package from the Integration Suite Discover portal

 - I configured the correct SuccessFactors API server URL, host alias, gave it a custom base path, etc.

- I applied the 'SuccessFactors_OAuth2SAMLAssertion' policy template. It automatically created the 8 policies under the Target Endpoint - Preflow.

 

- I configured the SF User ID, Client ID, Company ID, Authentication Token url and the private key. All this was created from the SuccessFactors Employee central - Manage OAuth Applications transaction

- When I hit update and save the API proxy I get the below error - Unable to update API - An internal error occurred, contact SAP Administrator for Detail

 

The weirdest part is that, I followed the same steps to set up an OData API proxy to SuccessFactors and it works like a charm. I tested it out successfully. 

What do you guys think the issue is? 

Kriba


 ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Edit 1: (08-03-2025)

This error is not particular to the Authentication policy. I am getting this error no matter which policy I add - message logging, basic authentication, even a dummy java script with no code. 

 

 ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Edit 2: (08-03-2025)

I have a sneaky suspicion that this is an error that's happening when copying the CompundEMployee API from the Discover portal. See the below when I copy an odata from the discover portal. When I try to edit the APIs in the API designer, I see all the different entities on the left.  

But when I try to check the compound employee API, I get the error 'No operations defined in Spec!'

But when I go to the Discover portal and search for Employee Central APIs and select Compound Employee and then click on Documentation, I see the full list of Operations

 

So, this definitely is a back-end problem. It's not copying over the operations correctly.

entry.title: HTTPS Sender adapter in iFlow shows sometimes a 401 error
entry.content:
Hi

I have an iFlow with a HTTPS sender adapter. We created a specific role and assigned the role to this iFlow and a clientID (generated in BTP). In general everything is working fine. An external partner is calling the endpoint and sending messages (XML IDOCs).

However we now have a situation that a specific message (relatively large, size 1MB, XML IDOC) is not being processed by the HTTPS sender adapter.

When checking the Integration Suite Logs I see the following that a HTTP 401 is returned.

Also a successfull request is having 401s but will get a 200 in a next request and will continue.

How do I need to interpret this and how can this be solved?

Thanks

Ron

entry.title: Authentication methods CPI DS Agent
entry.content:
Hi ,

 

I need to use CPI DS agent to set up integration between on prem system and IBP . What all authentication methods does CPI DS agent support for its connectivity to on prem . I want to avoid maintaining the credential on DS  on the cloud .. Can we have a oAuth connection between the DS Agent in the DMZ and on prem system ?Is there any documentation that I could follow for this scenario ?

entry.title: Read email using HTTP adapter Microsoft Graph API
entry.content:
Hi Expert

I want to read email using HTTP adapter Microsoft Graph API. 

I can not use Mail adapter as based on validation after reading mail should go two archive folder  Process and NotDone.

In mail adapter we can configure only one archive folder.

Is it necessary to use OAuth2 Authorization Code?

if we use   OAuth2 Authorization Code ,  Redirect URL is mandatory, but our vendor is unable to add it as they can not create different APP registration for test and prod environment .

does anyone use HTTP adapter to read email. any pointer for configuration?

 

Thanks in advance

entry.title: SAP S/4 HANA Public CLoud - send SalesOrder (SAP_COM_0109) to Cloud Integration
entry.content:
Hello Community,

I would currently like to establish a technical connection between the SAP S/4 HANA Public Cloud and Cloud Integration. I would like to distribute SalesOrder information (SAP_COM_0109 - Service Sales Order (A2X)) to the cloud integration via an OData adapter. In the SAP S/4 HANA Public Cloud, I have made settings for Communication Arrangements, Communication Systems and Maintain Communication Users. How do I manually trigger a SalesOrder message from the S/4 HANA Public Cloud, which steps do I have to perform? What does the endpoint in the OData (EDMX file I pulled from “https://my420450-api.s4hana.cloud.sap/sap/opu/odata/sap/API_SALES_ORDER_SRV/$metadata”) adapter look like as well as the entity set (currently A_SalesOrder)?

Thanks in advance and best regards

Michael Seydler

entry.title: how to deploy databricks jdbc drivers in SAP Cloud Integration
entry.content:
Hi Team,

Could you please advise, if there is a feasibility to deploy databricks jdbc drivers in SAP Cloud Integration ?

If yes, please provide the steps. 

I have a requirement to connect databricks DB to extract the data using JDBC connection. I have downloaded the databricks drivers and I tried importing it under "Microsoft SQL Server" database Type, but its not accepting it and throwing error as "Invalid Entry".

Appreciate your inputs.

Thanks,

Varun

entry.title: How to create an event in SAP Build Automation to call Rest IFLOW in Cloud Integration
entry.content: Hello!   We have a project that is being created in SAP Build Automation that will read an email, transform the attachment, and make a REST call to a SAP Cloud Integration IFLOW with a .json body. The Destination seems to be working as we can pull in the list of IFLOW's available.  But we're struggling on how to put the remaining pieces together to complete the process flow.   All of the blogs that I've found to date appear to be from earlier versions as their links are no longer found.

entry.title: Download sales order attachments as a physical file (pdf,...)
entry.content:
Hello all
I have a requirement to download sales order attachments as a physical file (pdf,...). Is there a BAPI or API that can be used to do this. We will be doing it in CPI. Does anyone have any experience with this?

entry.title: IDOC metadata change on SAP backend - Cloud Integration
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

We are working on IDOC to IDOC scenario on BTP Cloud Integration(CI) where our receiver system is also another CI which is on another subaccount ...IDOC metadata has been changed on SAP backend.. we need to adapt the IDOC schema changes on Cloud Integration to synchronize the schema on both SAP Backend & CI.. should we ask SAP backend colleagues to share XSD (or) WSDL? which is better practice) if they share XSD/WSDL then should we also the share the same with receiver BTP CI system contact? 

Thanks,

Ramu.

entry.title: Technical RFC Integration On-Premise-EWM 9.5 to Cloud Public 25/02
entry.content:
Background / Sceanrio:

  • We are setting up an integration between our On-Premise SAP EWM 9.5 and our Cloud Public  S/4 HANA 25/02.
  • In this scenario the Cloud Public is the “ERP” System and integrates to the “External” EWM 9.5 which is hosted within our company network.
  • The communication between the two systems is based on RFC - Remote Function Calls (Communication Scenario 0247).
  • We managed to establish the RFC communication FROM the Cloud Public TO the On-Premise EWM 9.5. This means, Master Data can already be replicated FROM Cloud Public TO the EWM via CIF (using RFC calls) and we can transfer Orders (e. g. Inbound) FROM Cloud Public TO the EWM 9.5
  • To have a full function integration via RFC Calls between the two systems it must also be possible to make RFC Calls FROM the On-Premise EWM 9.5 TO the Cloud Public (e. g. to send the Outbound Delivery order after Goods Issue posting).
  • We tried to setup this direction as described e. g. in documentation 2VJ_Set-Up_EN_XX.pdf (Setting Up SAP EWM Integration - Inbound Processing from Supplier (2VJ)).

Now the problem we are facing:
One of the first steps in the Integration guidelines is to setup the RFC connection in the On-Premise system to reach out TO the Cloud Public System. In other words a communication from our company internal network towards the Cloud Public is to be established.

Whatever we tried to setup in the On-Premise EWM 9.5 and/or our Cloud Connector fails as we can not "reach" the Cloud Public system

Our Question

  1. Is it a MUST, to open the firewall / Internet security settings to allow network traffic from the On-Premise network to The SAP Cloud Public system?
    OR
  2. Do we have to establish the connection from On-Premise towards Cloud Public using special setup on the Cloud Connector / Within the BTP or something like a "Public Cloud Proxy server", etc. (if yes, can SAP provide a documentation on how to do so)?
